name: Hershey's Syrup Two-Tone Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 package Yellow light cake mix (about 18.25 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
Heat oven to 350°F. Spray a 15-1/2 x 10-1/2 x 1-1/8-inch baking pan with vegetable oil spray. In a large mixing bowl, blend the dry cake mix, butter granules, cream cheese, water, egg whites, almond extract (if desired), and vanilla until combined; spread evenly into prepared pan. Prepare Hershey's Syrup Topper: In a small mixing bowl, beat 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1 cup sugar, 1/2 teaspoon baking powder, 1 cup Hershey's Syrup, and 4 egg whites until combined.
Pour the prepared Hershey's Syrup Topping evenly over the vanilla batter in the prepared pan. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack. Serve with whipped topping and additional chocolate syrup, if desired. Cover; refrigerate leftover cake. Makes 18 servings.
